feed.title: Combined summary - [BIP Proposal] Hybrid SPHINCS+ / secp256k1 Key Derivation for Quantum-Resistant Paper Wallets

entry.title: Combined summary - [BIP Proposal] Hybrid SPHINCS+ / secp256k1 Key Derivation for Quantum-Resistant Paper Wallets
entry.summary: A new proposal for a Bitcoin Improvement Proposal (BIP) has been introduced, focusing on creating a quantum-resistant key derivation scheme for Bitcoin wallets. The proposed method integrates the NIST-standardized SLH-DSA (SPHINCS+) with the 3’s Company HE-SD (High-Entropy Seed Distillation) algorithm, utilizing SPX-QEC for cleaning up processes. This combination aims to derive secp256k1 private keys and standard Bitcoin addresses, including legacy and BIP-341 Taproot addresses. Notably, the entire process is designed to be executed offline on low-end hardware like Raspberry Pi, making it accessible and practical for everyday use with popular platforms such as Electrum, bitaddress.org, and Bitcoin Core. The primary goal of this hybrid approach is to serve as a transitional solution for securing Bitcoin transactions against potential quantum computer threats, ensuring that wallet keys remain secure while broader consensus changes towards quantum security are developed. The reference implementation and complete code for this BIP can be found on GitHub at [this link](https://github.com/DigiMancer3D/bip-xxx-sphincs-hybrid), along with the draft text of the BIP at [another link](https://github.com/DigiMancer3D/bip-xxx-sphincs-hybrid/blob/main/bip-xxx.mediawiki). However, this proposal has met with skepticism from other developers in the community. Criticisms include confusion over the terminology used, such as the SPX-QEC and its role within the scheme, and concerns about the code's clarity and safety. Doubts have been raised regarding the actual enhancement of entropy and security through the proposed method, with some pointing out that a secp256k1 keypair cannot be made quantum-resistant merely by altering entropy sources. Concerns about the potential for the code to hide vulnerabilities that could be exploited to steal funds were also expressed, leading to advisories against using the proposed tool for generating paper wallet keys. Developers and users are cautioned to vet and trust links and tools before usage thoroughly.
